experiment: Track B, rung B2 — heuristic router ablation: does dynamic routing beat the static DAG, net of cost?
substrate: Exp-5 to_wire/from_wire chain (architect -> to_wire -> from_wire), held-out grader accept.py; the wire convention is recoverable from an in-code to_legacy cue.

cost_aware.note: cost is entirely the sonnet architect; the free-arm workers cost $0

verdict: GATE CLEARED (GO): dynamic routing beats the static all-sonnet DAG net of cost — equal success (both at ceiling) at a 67.8% lower cost ($0.52 vs $1.618/trial). The free arm (mimo, oc-mimo) genuinely implemented the worker tasks correctly (to_wire/from_wire with the acct/cents/ts convention, verified from the after-files; all three tasks completed on their assigned runners; grader PASS 5/5).
mechanism_verified: Per-trial after-files show correct to_wire/from_wire with the recoverable convention; manifests confirm to_wire ran on mimo and from_wire on oc-mimo (both free, both status=completed). The win is a cost win: the free arm handled the mechanical worker tasks that all-sonnet was paying full price for.
honest_reading: A SIMPLE heuristic (role-based cost-aware) already beat static. Per the roadmap's own logic, that means the routing win here is 80%-shaped: you do not need a LEARNED coordinator to capture it. So B3's gate is not 'does dynamic beat static' (answered here, yes) but 'does a LEARNED router beat the HEURISTIC router?' The specific win is a cost win that holds BECAUSE the free arm was capable enough for THIS recoverable worker task; on harder worker tasks the free arm may fail (success loss). Knowing WHERE the free arm's capability boundary is (which tasks to send free vs mid) is the thing a learned policy could add over the fixed role-heuristic — that is B3's potential value, and its real gate.
limitations: One task, one substrate. N=5 on the cost-aware arm: success is at ceiling but not TOST-confirmed equivalent to the static 12/12 (a small success loss below the N=5 detection floor cannot be ruled out; the 68% cost margin is large enough that a modest success dip would still net-favor cost-aware, but that is not established). The free-arm workers' telemetry does not report num_turns/USD (free), so cost is architect-only by construction.
